title: Iran refuses to negotiate as U.S. intensifies military attacks for fifth day
summary: U.S. forces have struck Iranian targets multiple times, while Tehran refuses dialogue and reinforces its defense posture.

title: Nobel laureate Susumu Tonegawa dies at 86
summary: The Japanese scientist revolutionized immunology with the discovery of antibody diversity, becoming the first from his country to win the Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine.

title: Argentina defeats England and qualifies for the World Cup final
summary: The defending champions came from behind with two assists from Lionel Messi to seal their place in the final for the second consecutive time.

title: Nikkei index falls more than 2,000 points on tech sector fears
summary: Tokyo stock market suffers heavy losses dragged down by selling of tech stocks and geopolitical uncertainty.

title: Yokohama leads against Tokaidai Sagami in Kanagawa high school baseball tournament
summary: The fourth-round match between two youth baseball powerhouses draws long lines of fans and maintains high anticipation.

title: Prime Minister pushes for consumption tax reduction despite criticism
summary: Takashi defends the tax cut as an opportunity to ease household finances, while the opposition calls for a deeper debate.

title: Parliament extends its session amid opposition protests
summary: The ruling coalition seeks to pass key bills before the deadline, while minority parties criticize the lack of consensus.

title: Route for Hokuriku Shinkansen extension with new station in Kyoto finalized
summary: The route will go through Katsuragawa Station and the Obama-Kyoto route, aiming to improve regional connectivity.

title: International visits to Japan fall for first time in five years
summary: The decline in Chinese tourism, which halved, affects the record visitor numbers in the first half of the year.

title: Bear attacks on homes increase in rural areas of Japan
summary: A bear has attacked the same house six times in a week, as authorities seek to protect residents.

title: Ex-nurse arrested for injecting feces into patients refuses to testify
summary: The suspect searched for information on whether the injection could be lethal, according to internet search records.

title: Cyberattack on Nichirei causes shortage of frozen food in supermarkets
summary: The failure in logistics systems affects chains like Aeon and Don Quijote, which report product shortages.

title: Mayor of Oharu investigated for entering former candidate's office without permission
summary: Police question the local politician for possible trespassing during the election campaign.

title: Lower House passes bill to create a 'subcapital' in Osaka
summary: The ruling party seeks to establish a secondary capital, but the opposition criticizes the speed of the process.

title: Debate over reform of the Imperial House Law intensifies in Parliament
summary: The government and opposition disagree on the need to change imperial succession rules and the scope of the reform.

title: Zelensky appoints new prime minister and strengthens military cooperation with EU
summary: Ukraine changes head of government to prepare for winter and agrees to joint drone production with the European Union.

title: Wall Street closes higher driven by inflation data and rate expectations
summary: Major U.S. indices rise on the possibility that the Federal Reserve will halt interest rate hikes.

title: Bank of Japan minutes reveal divisions over negative rate policy
summary: BOJ members discussed whether the measure was a failure or a necessary experience for the Japanese economy.

title: Opposition sharply questions prime minister in leaders' debate
summary: Takashi avoided answering questions about his leadership and electoral system reform, drawing criticism from the opposition.

title: Extreme heat wave forces rescheduling of events across Japan
summary: Authorities urge the public to take measures against the heat as temperatures reach record levels.
